chapter_title.en: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification

text: 306 - وَقَالَ اَلْحُسَيْنُ بْنُ أَبِي اَلْعَلاَءِ لِلصَّادِقِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: مَا ثَوَابُ مَنْ أَخَذَ مِنْ شَارِبِهِ وَ قَلَّمَ أَظْفَارَهُ فِي كُلِّ جُمُعَةٍ قَالَ «لاَ يَزَالُ مُطَهَّراً إِلَى اَلْجُمُعَةِ اَلْأُخْرَى.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.306 - Al-Hussain ibn Abi al-'Ala asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as):
'What is the reward for someone who trims their moustache and cuts their nails every Jumu’ah?'
Imam (as) replied: 'They will remain purified until the next Jumu’ah.'
